disability_rights.body_en: Equality & Non-Discrimination (Section 6) PwDs are guaranteed full equality before the law, including equal protection and access to goods, services, and facilities. Special measures or support services implemented to help achieve this equality are legally protected and do not constitute discrimination. Legal & Financial Capacity (Section 7) PwDs have the right to be recognized as persons before the law with equal legal capacity. They have full rights to own and inherit property, manage their own finances, and access loans, mortgages, and credit, with access to support services if needed to exercise these rights. Marriage & Family Rights (Section 8) Adult PwDs have the right to freely marry and form a family. They retain control over their sexual and reproductive health and cannot be separated from their children solely on the basis of their disability. Their property rights are protected before, during, and after marriage. Right to Privacy (Section 9) PwDs are protected against arbitrary interference in their personal privacy, home, family, and communications. Public and private institutions must keep personal health and rehabilitation records strictly confidential, sharing them only with the express consent of the individual. Rights of Women with Disabilities (Section 10) Women with disabilities are granted specific protections to ensure they enjoy their human rights equally. Key rights include equal participation in decision-making, protection and support against sexual and gender-based violence (GBV), control over their fertility and reproductive health, the right to keep their children, and overall access to development and empowerment.
